issues.title: Time-conversion constants duplicated across 3 feature files
issues.description: MINUTES_PER_HOUR, SECONDS_PER_MINUTE, and MS_PER_SECOND are independently defined in three separate files. packages/shared already has a constants/time.ts file that currently only exposes QUERY-related constants; these time-unit primitives should live there instead.
issues.fix: Add MS_PER_SECOND, SECONDS_PER_MINUTE, MINUTES_PER_HOUR, and HOURS_PER_DAY to packages/shared/src/constants/time.ts and export them. Then replace the three local definitions with imports from 'shared'.

issues.title: Duplicate useSellerProfiles hook in store and payments
issues.description: useSellerProfiles(sellerIds: string[]) exists in two apps with the same purpose: fetch display names from user_profiles for a list of seller IDs. The store version inlines the Supabase query directly; the payments version delegates to fetchSellerProfiles. Additionally, payments/shared/infrastructure has a third helper, fetchUserDisplayNames, that performs the identical DB query pattern. Three implementations of the same data-fetch logic.
issues.fix: Consolidate the Supabase query into a single function in packages/shared (or packages/api) and have both apps import it. The hook wrappers can stay per-app (different staleTime, different error behavior), but the underlying DB fetch should be shared.
issues.note: The store version dedupes IDs with Set before querying; the payments version does not. Verify this difference is intentional before merging.

issues.title: validateUuid duplicated in admin and payments API routes
issues.description: validateUuid (UUID regex validation throwing on mismatch) is defined independently in apps/admin/src/app/api/admin/_shared/adminRest.ts and apps/payments/src/shared/infrastructure/adminRestClient.ts. Both use a slightly different constant name (UUID_PATTERN vs UUID_REGEX) but the same regex and identical throw logic.
issues.fix: Move validateUuid and the UUID regex into packages/shared (e.g., packages/shared/src/utils/validateUuid.ts) and import from there in both apps. The admin version uses a different error message constant (INVALID_PAYLOAD_ERROR) — align error messages when consolidating.

issues.title: isValidCartItem logic duplicated across store and payments
issues.description: isValidCartItem (type-guard checking id/price_usd/quantity) is implemented in apps/store/src/features/cart/application/cartValidation.ts and again inline (private) in apps/payments/src/features/checkout/infrastructure/cartCookie.ts. The payments version additionally checks price_cop. The logic is nearly identical and serves the same purpose: parsing the shared cart cookie.
issues.fix: Since the cart cookie is a shared contract between store and payments, consider placing the CartItem shape definition and its validator in packages/shared (or a dedicated cart-cookie package). Both apps can then import one canonical validator.
issues.note: The payments validator checks price_cop in addition to price_usd. Reconcile which fields are truly required before merging.

issues.title: ProtectedRoute wrapper files are identical in every app
issues.description: Five apps (store, admin, payments, studio, playground) each have an identical one-liner ProtectedRoute.tsx that just calls createProtectedRoute(appUrls.auth). The file content is 100% the same across all five.
issues.fix: This is a minor duplication acceptable under KISS — each file is one line and the local import of appUrls is intentional (each app may have a different auth URL). No action required unless the pattern grows in complexity. Acceptable as-is.

patterns.name: display_name ?? email fallback pattern
patterns.description: The pattern `profile.display_name ?? profile.email.split('@')[0]` or `profile.display_name ?? profile.email` appears in 10+ places across store, auth, studio, admin, and payments. It is already partially abstracted in fetchUserDisplayNames (payments) but the presentation layer still duplicates it inline. Not flagged as a rule violation because individual uses are single expressions, but worth noting as a candidate for a shared formatDisplayName utility in packages/shared.
